prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo beta-rich protein (60–80 residues) that disrupts dimerization of NSP5 main protease (Mpro) from SARS-CoV-2 by occluding the dimer interface without acting as a catalytic mimic. The designed protein should achieve sub-micromolar binding to the Mpro dimer interface. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion backbone generation targeting the Mpro dimer interface residues, (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design with hydrophobic burial constraints, (3) structure prediction with ESMFold and binding free energy estimation. Discuss cross-reactivity with human cysteine proteases.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: NSP5 main protease; organism: SARS-CoV-2; catalogue version v1.0
